question: Would the top of Mount Fuji stick out of the Sea of Japan?
context: The average depth of the Sea of Japan is  5,748 feet (1,752 metres) and its maximum depth is 12,276 feet (3,742 metres)
Mount Fuji is 3,776.24 metres (12,389.2 ft) tall.
response:
<reasoning>
The maximum depth of the Sea of Japan is 3,742 metres.
Mount Fuji is 3,776.24 metres tall.
3,776.24 metres is greater than 3,742 metres.
The top of Mount Fuji would stick out of the Sea of Japan.
<answer> Yes
claims: The maximum depth of the Sea of Japan is 3,742 metres
The top of Mount Fuji would stick out of the Sea of Japan

labeled_claims.text: The maximum depth of the Sea of Japan is 3,742 metres
The top of Mount Fuji would stick out of the Sea of Japan
